message.content: Adversaries may exploit the AuthorizationExecuteWithPrivileges API to escalate privileges by prompting users for credentials, enabling them to perform operations with root privileges. This API, although deprecated, remains functional in the latest macOS versions and does not validate the source or integrity of the requesting program. Attackers can leverage this to install malicious software and persistence mechanisms by tricking users into granting elevated privileges, often through Masquerading. Additionally, legitimate programs using this API can be modified to execute malicious actions. The potential impact includes unauthorized root access, leading to system compromise and persistent threats. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring for unusual use of the API, educating users on recognizing suspicious prompts, and employing application whitelisting to prevent unauthorized software execution.
